textPlain: I know it's overused and for example in MacOS introduces way too much white space, but people like rounded shapes. They make people more comfortable and think the content is simpler than when we get hard angles. It's been tested in so many research papers and the basically all agree - people like bouba more than kiki, whatever the context is.

My contribution to the genre: negative border radius
Let's make the (digital) world sharperhttps://x.com/TheOisinMoran/status/1846417247075459235

My gripe with border-radius is that it makes everything look the same.I'd really like more corner types.  Back in the tables and sliced images days, we'd have all manner of neat angular borders, and tons of variety.  Now it's all squircles everywhere.

I wonder whether they want to fuck the Fernández–Guasti squircle, Lamé's special quartic, or both, and whether their desires extend to higher-dimensional sphubes.  Possibly their squigonometry just can't handle such curves.Is there a Bresenham-style algorithm similar to the midpoint algorithm for roundrects that can produce other kinds of squircles?
If we’re going to fuck something, it should be something more consequential like JIRA.https://ifuckinghatejira.com/
